Outside party lines, most groups favor photo ID, including Hispanic voters (85% to register, 76% to vote) and voters under age 30 (82%, 75%).

Looking toward the fall midterms, most are broadly confident eligible voters will be able to vote (76%), though fewer feel sure ineligible voters will be prevented from doing so (61%). Confidence in eligible ballot access is high across party lines, while Republicans are less certain than Democrats and independents about ineligible voting.

FOX NEWS POLL: AN EARLY LOOK AT THE GEORGIA SENATE RACE

Nearly two-thirds (64%) favor universal mail-in voting, holding steady since September 2025. Partisan divisions persist, as nearly 9 in 10 Democrats and 7 in 10 independents support mail-in voting, while about 6 in 10 Republicans oppose it.

Despite majority backing, views are divided on the accuracy of mail-in ballots. A narrow 51% majority is confident they will be counted accurately, while 48% disagree. Two-thirds of Democrats trust the accuracy of the count, while roughly 6 in 10 Republicans and independents think it’s unlikely.

Most also favor counting ballots postmarked by Election Day, even if they arrive later. Seven in 10 support the practice, including large majorities of Democrats (88%) and independents (74%), while closer to half of Republicans oppose (53%).

In June, the Supreme Court upheld a Mississippi law permitting late mail-in ballots to be counted if postmarked by Election Day.

Regardless of voting method, more express confidence that votes will be counted accurately in their state (64%) than at the national level (53%). Democrats trust both, while more Republicans and independents are assured by state tallies. In addition, by a 2-to-1 margin, more voters think state governments (68%) rather than the federal government (31%) should have election authority, a view shared across all major political groups.

Overall, 6 in 10 are confident the final vote count will be fair and accurate, even if completing the tally takes several days after Election Day. Roughly 7 in 10 Democrats are confident, while more than half of Republicans are not. Independents are divided (52% confident, 47% not).

CLICK HERE FOR CROSSTABS AND TOPLINE

Conducted July 17-20, 2026, under the direction of Beacon Research (D) and Shaw & Company Research (R), this Fox News survey includes interviews with a sample of 1,003 registered voters randomly selected from a national voter file. Respondents spoke with live interviewers on landlines (70) and cellphones (681) or completed the survey online after receiving a text (252). Results based on the full sample have a margin of sampling error of ±3 percentage points. The survey includes an oversample of 200 voters aged 18-29, bringing the sample size for that subgroup to 350 (±5). Sampling error for results among subgroups is higher. In addition to sampling error, question wording and order can influence results. Weights are generally applied to age, race, education, and area variables to ensure the demographics are representative of the registered voter population. Sources for developing weight targets include the most recent American Community Survey, Fox News Voter Analysis, and voter file data.

item.title: Lynx star Kayla McBride becomes latest to speak out on WNBA 3-point contest drama: ‘Half a-- invite’
item.description: Minnesota Lynx star Kayla McBride said she received a "half-a--" invite to the WNBA 3-point contest via text 48 hours through her agent before the event.
item.content:encoded:
The WNBA may have a communication problem.

Minnesota Lynx star Kayla McBride, like New York Liberty star Sabrina Ionescu, was forced to set the record straight regarding her absence from the WNBA’s 3-point contest and aired out her thoughts on the WNBA’s "half-a--" invitation.

"Also, for those that are SO concerned. I received a half a-- invite to the 3 pt contest, thru my agent, in a text randomly 48 hours ago..not officially," McBride posted on her Instagram Story on Wednesday.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

"Sooo posting things like i’m afraid of shooting smoke is weird and if you didn’t hear it from me prob shouldn’t believe it ," she wrote with a shrug emoji.

She said the attention should instead be on the competition’s participants: Portland Fire’s Bridget Carleton, Dallas Wings’ Azzi Fudd, Seattle Storm’s Natisha Hiedeman, Atlanta Dream’s Rhyne Howard, Toronto Tempo’s Marina Mabrey and Golden State Valkyries’ Janelle Salaün.

LIBERTY'S SABRINA IONESCU ADDRESSES ABSENCE FROM WNBA 3-POINT CONTEST: 'NEVER OFFICIALLY GOT INVITED'

"We should focus on the great shooters in it already!!! because there’s some killas in there. good luck to all those participating and when y'all start doing shooting contests coming off pin downs hit me," McBride posted.

The 34-year-old has participated in three previous 3-point contests but has never won the event. She most recently participated in 2024.

Ionescu is the reigning champion and won two of the three 3-point contests. She said she never got officially invited and took issue with the idea she declined to participate.

McBride is in the midst of a career year, averaging 18.2 points per game through 28 games, which matches her career-best output from 2018.

The event is set to take place on Friday at 8 p.m. ET and will be without McBride, Ionescu and Caitlin Clark.

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.

item.title: Heidi Klum ditches bikini top, covers up with potato chip bag on beach vacation
item.description: Heidi Klum's sultry beach video shows her using a bag of potato chips as a topless cover-up in a playful nod to her new snack brand collaboration.
item.content:encoded:
Heidi Klum is serving up sunshine and a cheeky snack.

The supermodel shared a series of beach videos on Instagram showing herself soaking up the tropical scenery alongside one of her beloved dogs.

In one video, Klum lounged on the sand wearing an oversized woven sun hat, dark sunglasses and zebra-print bikini bottoms while using a bag of Chipsfrisch potato chips to cover her bare chest.

GISELE BÜNDCHEN CELEBRATES BIRTHDAY IN LEOPARD-PRINT BIKINI AS SHE REFLECTS ON GRATITUDE

"Glazed ham on the beach," Klum captioned the video. The chips appeared to be glazed ham-flavored.

The model's comments section lit up with fire and heart emojis. 

"Baaaaabe sooo 🔥🔥🔥 🌶️🌶️🌶️," Nicole Scherzinger wrote, while others called Klum an "icon" and praised her beauty. 

One fan wrote, "The chips got the best product placement of 2026."

In another video, Klum was seen wading through crystal-clear turquoise water with her fluffy white dog.

She lifted the pup high above her head toward the sky with her back to the camera. The former Victoria's Secret model eventually turned and appeared topless while sharing a cuddle with her pet as her wide-brimmed hat shaded much of her face.

The Instagram post simply read, "Finally," alongside an island and paw-print emoji.

SYDNEY SWEENEY SHARES BIKINI AND UNDERWEAR PHOTOS IN INSTAGRAM DUMP FROM AUSTRALIA TRIP

The carefree beach outing comes after Klum partnered with German snack brand funny-frisch to launch a special line of Chipsfrisch by Heidi Klum potato chips, making the bag she used as a playful cover-up a fitting nod to the collaboration.

Klum's getaway comes just weeks after the supermodel opened up about embracing her body and tuning out Hollywood's growing weight-loss craze.

SUPERMODEL SLAMS GLP-1 TREND, SAYS WEIGHT LOSS SHOTS ARE A 'SMACK IN THE FACE' TO BODY POSITIVITY MOVEMENT

Earlier this month, the supermodel revealed she has no interest in jumping on the Ozempic bandwagon, saying husband Tom Kaulitz helped her ditch the fashion industry's long-standing pressure to be thinner.

"I'm not interested at all," Klum told Us Weekly when asked about weight-loss drugs.

LIKE WHAT YOU’RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

Instead, Klum said she's embraced the changes that have come with age.

"I've gained weight over the years. I'm no longer a size 24 jean. But I think with age, we look better when we're not as skinny."

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

The 53-year-old said it was Kaulitz who first challenged the modeling world's beauty standards.

"My husband was the first one who pointed that out. He said, 'You should eat more; you would look better if you had more meat on your bones.' I was like, 'What?' This is not something you're told in the industry, ever, because you're always supposed to be skinnier than you are."

Klum admitted she eventually agreed with her husband.

"When I look back at photos, I'm like, 'He's right!' Proportion-wise, I look better bigger."

Klum and the German guitarist secretly tied the knot in 2019 before celebrating with a lavish wedding aboard the superyacht Christina O. off the coast of Capri, Italy.

item.title: Suspect arrested after allegedly yelling ‘Allahu Akbar,' stabbing 2 men in NYC attack
item.description: NYPD arrested a suspect after two men were reportedly stabbed near Central Park West in Manhattan. Officials are investigating whether the attacks were antisemitic in nature.
item.content:encoded:
A suspect is in custody after allegedly yelling ‘Allahu Akbar’ while stabbing two men in what officials are describing as an "antisemetic" attack in Manhattan's Upper West Side Thursday afternoon.

The New York City Police Department (NYPD) said one of the victims is Jewish and that witnesses reported hearing the suspect yell "Allahu Akbar" during both assaults.

Investigators are evaluating whether the reported attacks should be classified as a hate crime and identified the suspect as Raul Morales, 51, in connection with both assaults.

"Detectives are continuing to work the case and determine motive, but according to victim and witness statements, Morales yelled ‘Allahu Akbar’ during both attacks," Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch said. 

NYC ANTISEMITIC INCIDENTS NEARLY TRIPLE DESPITE OTHER CRIMES REACHING RECORD LOWS

The stabbings happened near West 84th Street and Central Park West, an area with a large Jewish community.

Hours after the attack, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who recently called on the federal government to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, said he had been briefed on what he described as "horrifying stabbings" and noted the victims were an Asian man and a Jewish man.

"According to witness and victim statements, the perpetrator yelled 'Allahu Akbar' during both attacks," Mamdani said. "The NYPD responded immediately and arrested Raul Morales in connection with both assaults — and I am grateful to the officers for their swift response."

Mamdani issued his statement roughly two hours after City Council Speaker Julie Menin and about 45 minutes after Gov. Kathy Hochul.

According to the NYPD, the victims were stabbed in what appeared to be two separate attacks that occurred near one another.

A 57-year-old man was reportedly attacked near West 84th Street and Central Park West just after 1:30 p.m. local time.

CALLS FOR US TO DO MORE AS ANTISEMITIC ACTS SKYROCKET IN EUROPE: 'ENORMOUSLY PAINFUL'

Officials said officers found him conscious and alert with a stab wound to his back. He was taken to a local hospital in stable condition.

Investigators later found a 50-year-old man nearby with a stab wound to his torso. He was also conscious and alert, and was taken to a nearby hospital in stable condition.

Initial NYPD findings suggest that mental health may have been a factor. 

"While the perpetrator has no known mental health history with the NYPD, the initial investigation suggests that mental health may have been a factor," Tisch said. 

WABC reported that police are investigating whether the suspect used a knife or a screwdriver during the alleged attacks.

"I am absolutely sickened and furious by what appears to be an antisemitic stabbing on the Upper West Side," Menin wrote in a post on X.

"While we await additional details to be confirmed, we know that what is happening in our city is abhorrent," Menin said. "The fear in the Jewish community is real. Every New Yorker deserves to feel safe in their neighborhood, regardless of their faith or identity."

"We must remain united in condemning antisemitism and ensuring those responsible are held fully accountable," she added. "I thank the NYPD for its swift response in apprehending the assailant."

New York Assembly member Micah Lasher, a Jewish representative of the area, said he is also in contact with investigators.

"I am in touch with the NYPD and community leaders about the stabbing of two individuals on the Upper West Side today," Lasher said. "The NYPD responded immediately and has apprehended a suspect. I am praying for the survival and recovery of the victims and will provide more information as I have it."

Hochul posted on X saying that she is "disgusted by what appears to be another senseless attack on New Yorkers simply because they’re Jewish."

"The strength of New York has always been our ability to live together across every faith and background," Hochul added. "Anyone who tries to tear that apart will be met with the full force of the law. New York will stand with our Jewish community today, tomorrow, and always."

Minister of Foreign Affairs of the State of Israel Gideon Sa'ar added that he was "appalled but sadly not surprised" by the incident, blaming Mamdani for creating "a climate in which Jews are targeted." 

"I am appalled by the horrific antisemitic stabbing attack in New York. Appalled but sadly not surprised," he said in a post on X. 

"This violence did not happen in a vacuum. Months of incitement against the Jewish state from Mamdani have helped create a climate in which Jews are targeted. Words have consequences. Mamdani must end the incitement, confront antisemitism with resolve, and act now to protect the city’s Jewish community."

Mamdani's office, along with the FBI, did not immediately respond to further inquiries from Fox News Digital.

This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

item.title: USA Today columnist who slammed Caitlin Clark declared US greed would ruin World Cup
item.description: Nancy Armour called America hateful and greedy before the World Cup, but record attendance, interantional goodwill and $20 billion in spending told a different story.
item.content:encoded:
USA Today columnist Nancy Armour, who faced criticism Thursday over her commentary about WNBA star Caitlin Clark, had declared last month before the World Cup began that American greed and hostility had already cost the United States its opportunity to showcase the country, citing ticket prices and entry restrictions.

"The U.S. men’s national team could make a historically deep run and the tournament could be filled with wondrous goals and compelling games," Armour said. "It will not change the fact that we have shown ourselves to be a hateful and greedy nation, one where leaders are only willing to welcome a world as they want to see it rather than as it exists, and the love of the game is exploited for top dollar."

Armour predicted that the tournament would be remembered for the country's conduct instead of the competition and its ability to bring people together.

TRUMP ADMIN TAUNTS LEFT FOR ‘PRAYING’ FOR WORLD CUP DISASTER AS DOT TOUTS RECORD NUMBERS

"That will be the legacy, and the shame, of this World Cup," Armour said. "This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to showcase the best of the United States and embrace the joy that soccer brings the rest of the world and, instead, we chose to be ugly Americans."

The columnist criticized visa restrictions affecting Iran's team and the denial of entry to Somali referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan. She argued that U.S. entry restrictions conflicted with the country’s responsibility to welcome international participants and fans.

"The U.S. is supposed to be a World Cup host, not a World Cup bouncer," Armour said.

Armour’s columns received renewed attention after Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy and other critics condemned her on Thursday for invoking Emmett Till while arguing that Clark’s conduct toward WNBA officials endangered Black and queer players.

"It seems there is a White nationalist element to Clark’s fandom that is deeply, deeply problematic," Armour said.

WORLD CUP CHAOS AVOIDED BY TRUMP MOVES HE DIDN’T MAKE, ‘EQUALLY AS IMPORTANT’ AS ACTIONS TAKEN: TRAVEL EXEC

The FIFA World Cup concluded Sunday after 104 matches, including 78 in the United States. Nearly 7 million people attended matches, breaking the tournament attendance record, while FIFA reported 99.7% capacity through the semifinals. More than 8.5 million people visited official fan festivals across the three host countries, according to FIFA.

The final between Spain and Argentina drew nearly 63 million U.S. viewers across Fox, Telemundo and Peacock, Reuters reported. Fox averaged 7.74 million viewers throughout the tournament, up 116% from 2022, while Telemundo averaged 6.3 million, a 143% increase.

Bank of America estimated that the World Cup generated approximately $40 billion in global economic activity, with about $20 billion occurring in the United States. The bank found faster spending growth in host cities, particularly at hotels, restaurants, bars and stores, The Independent reported.

CDC REVEALS MASSIVE BEHIND-THE-SCENES PUBLIC HEALTH OPERATION THAT KEPT FIFA WORLD CUP FANS SAFE

"It’s having this on-the-ground economic impact, and that spending is going into what we call bricks-and-mortar — going to bars and restaurants and things like that — not necessarily only the people in the stadium," Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan said in a CBS interview with Margaret Brennan.

Houston recorded more than 439,000 international airport arrivals during the tournament, up more than 2% from the previous year, while hotel revenue rose nearly 15%. Visitors interviewed by Axios praised the city's hospitality, food and diversity.

"People arrived in the United States, they arrived in Texas, they arrived in Houston, and it was a much different experience than what maybe they had originally thought, and in a very, very favorable way," Houston First CEO Michael Heckman said.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE COVERAGE OF MEDIA AND CULTURE

A U.S. Travel Association survey of 2,205 adults found that 81% felt proud seeing international visitors enjoy the country, 86% felt joy watching them experience America, and 70% supported hosting additional global events. Half said positive visitor experiences build international goodwill and cooperation, according to the survey.

"The World Cup reintroduced America to the world. Our best ambassadors aren’t politicians or headlines — they’re the Americans visitors met, the communities they explored and the people who made them feel welcome," U.S. Travel Association President Geoff Freeman said.

"That’s a win for every American."
